synthesized text: I'm standing in front of my physics class, trying to come up with a way to explain the concept of kinetic energy to my students. I glance out the window and see a group of kids playing soccer during their lunch break. That's when it hits me - what if I use the soccer ball as an example? "Alright, class," I say, "let's talk about kinetic energy. Imagine one of those soccer players kicking the ball with all their might. As the ball flies through the air, what kind of energy does it have?" I pause for a moment, surveying the room to make sure my students are engaged. "That's right, it has kinetic energy. But what happens when the ball starts to slow down and eventually comes to a stop? Where does that kinetic energy go?" I ask, encouraging my students to think critically about the problem.
label: instruction
